etymology.description: semantic 音(音) (yīn, "sound") + phonetic 鄉(鄉) (xiāng) -> sound
etymology.interpretation: The character 向(向) (xiàng, "direction") provides the phonetic component, while 音(音) (yīn, "sound") denotes the meaning, together forming the concept of 'sound.'
etymology.simplification: Simplified to 响 by removing 音 (yīn, "sound") and retaining only the outline with the phonetic 鄉(鄉) (xiāng)

examples.chinese: 铃声响起(鈴聲響起)，我立刻接了电话(我立刻接了電話)。
examples.pinyin: Língshēng xiǎngqǐ, wǒ lìkè jiē le diànhuà.
examples.english: The ringtone started, and I immediately answered the phone.

examples.chinese: 大炮的声响震耳欲聋(大炮的聲響震耳欲聾)。
examples.english: The sound of the cannon was deafening.

examples.chinese: 雨水拍打着窗户(雨水拍打著窗戶)，声音非常清晰(聲音非常清晰)。
examples.pinyin: Yǔshuǐ pāidǎ zhe chuānghù, shēngyīn fēicháng qīngxī.
examples.english: The rain was hitting the window, and the sound was very clear.
